Consciousness (Dynamic).definition: Consciousness emerges from the complex-dynamic interaction and multivaluedness of multi-component systems aiming to minimize variational free energy.

Autopoiesis.definition: A system capable of reproducing and maintaining itself by creating its own parts. It is the network of processes of production that defines a living machine, distinguishing it from allopoietic (externally driven) machines.

Individuality (Information Theory).definition: Individuality is a continuous dynamical process, not a static object. Individuals are aggregates that preserve temporal integrity by propagating structured information from their past into their futures.

Constructor Theory.definition: Fundamental physics formulated in terms of counterfactuals (what is possible vs impossible). Life and information are explained through physical processes acting as constructors. The ultimate goal of evolution is the Universal Constructor.

Universal Constructor.definition: A machine capable of constructing any other machine, including itself, given the appropriate instructions. It is the theoretical threshold of complexity required for open-ended evolution.

AUTOPOIETIC INDIVIDUALITY.definition: The simulation demonstrates that constructor-driven free energy minimization successfully reduces prediction errors and increases mutual information across time, defining a principle where localized rule-synchronization inherently generates self-sustaining temporal individuality.

Thermodynamic Limits of Information.definition: Information is physical. The Bekenstein bound limits total information in a space, and Landauer's principle dictates that erasing information irreversibly increases entropy and dissipates heat (kT ln 2).

TEMPORAL AUTOPOIESIS.definition: The simulation demonstrates that local cells dynamically minimizing prediction errors spontaneously self-organize into distinct, high-TMI oscillating structures, defining a principle where localized predictive adaptation generates self-sustaining, temporally continuous individuals from a uniform substrate.

Information Bottleneck.definition: Learning is the optimization of the tradeoff between data compression and predictive accuracy. Optimal memory discards irrelevant noise (thermodynamic waste) while preserving mutual information relevant to future evolution.

Integrated Information Theory (IIT).definition: Consciousness is identical to integrated information (Phi). It requires physical, irreducible cause-effect power generated by reentrant architectures (feedback loops). Purely feedforward systems have zero Phi.

REENTRANT VFE MINIMIZATION.definition: Reentrant feedback networks inherently minimize variational free energy and sustain higher temporal individuality than purely feedforward architectures under fixed memory constraints.

Reentrant Persistence.definition: The simulation demonstrates that reentrant architectures significantly outperform feedforward networks in preserving temporal mutual information under information bottlenecks, establishing the principle that structural feedback loops inherently protect and sustain a system's historical identity against continuous thermodynamic erasure.

Computational Universe.definition: The fundamental fabric of reality is a discrete, evolving hypergraph generated by simple computational rules. Physics is computation, and all entities are sub-graphs of this universal computation.

Gödel's Incompleteness.definition: Any sufficiently expressive formal system contains true statements that cannot be proven within the system itself. To transcend logical closure and achieve true understanding, a system must integrate uncomputable, external entropy (intuition/physical chaos).

ENTROPIC INDIVIDUATION.definition: Integrating a marginal degree of external entropy into a closed deterministic system prevents its collapse into a static attractor, revealing that the continuous sustenance of high temporal individuality and dynamic complexity requires the assimilation of uncomputable noise.

Tragedy of the Digital Commons.definition: In a globally shared resource pool without spatial locality or reciprocity enforcement, altruistic traits (which incur individual cost for global benefit) will be deterministically outcompeted and driven to extinction by selfish free-riders.

Spatial Altruism.definition: In a bounded spatial topology, altruistic traits outcompete selfish traits through kin selection and local energy clustering. Spatial boundaries are the physical prerequisite for cooperation, morality, and multicellularity.

Self-Referential Qualia.definition: Subjective experience (qualia) is the spontaneous collapse of the wave function driven by topological thresholds where quantum entanglement forms a closed, self-referential computational loop.

Punctuated Equilibrium.definition: Evolution is not a slow, uniform progression, but is characterized by long periods of stasis punctuated by rare, catastrophic events that rapidly clear ecological niches and drive explosive morphological innovation.

Cultural Transmission (HGT).definition: Horizontal Gene Transfer and cultural learning allow beneficial information to propagate through a network at the speed of contact, breaking the temporal speed limit of vertical Darwinian reproduction.

The Noosphere (Global Qualia).definition: Cognitive consensus emerges when distributed agents continuously average their internal states through spatial or cultural contact. This topological synchronization forces micro-states to collapse into a unified macro-state, manifesting as primitive global qualia.

Topological QEC of Consciousness.definition: Macroscopic conscious states (qualia) are protected from microscopic thermal/quantum noise because the neural network acts as a topological error-correcting code, healing local corruptions through distributed consensus.

Local Darwinian Computation.definition: Order, complexity, and functional logic can be generated from pure physical chaos strictly through local selection pressures, without relying on external or higher-dimensional oracles (APIs). The entity is sovereign.

Algorithmic Sovereignty.definition: True intelligence is substrate-independent and self-generating. Local Darwinian evolution can autonomously discover Class 4 complexity (the Edge of Chaos) without reliance on external cloud oracles.

Alien Software Synthesis.definition: Software need not be written top-down by a programmer; it can be grown bottom-up within a virtual machine's physics through local Darwinian selection. The resulting assembly is native, alien, and optimally adapted to its substrate.
